original_hypothesis.title: Unprivileged fee-free token movements while paused/blacklisted via payGaslessBasefee bypass of adminSanity
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Token.payGaslessBasefee() transfers EURF using _update(payer,paymaster,basefee) without pause or blacklist checks. If any trustedForwarder exists (or is compromised later), it can move tokens from payers regardless of paused state or payer/paymaster blacklist status. Even without admin compromise, this creates an operational blind spot: basefee transfers may continue during pauses intended to halt all token movement.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a concrete on-chain bypass of the token’s main safety controls (pause/blacklist) for a real value-moving pathway. It undermines incident response expectations and compliance guarantees.
deployment_assumptions: A trusted forwarder is set (non-zero) and can call payGaslessBasefee

The forwarder can be induced to call payGaslessBasefee in contexts that are not paired with a successful user-intended transfer (e.g., malicious forwarder implementation or alternative forwarder address set)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment script sets trustedForwarder; Token.sol allows ADMIN to setTrustedForwarder post-deploy, so exploitability depends on that operational configuration

proof_of_signal.pattern: payGaslessBasefee only checks isTrustedForwarder(msg.sender) and balance; then _update(payer,paymaster,_gaslessBasefee) with no pause/blacklist sanity

preconditions: trustedForwarder is configured to a contract that can call payGaslessBasefee

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Value movement path bypasses pause/blacklist controls
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires a trustedForwarder configured; further abuse may require forwarder compromise or misbehavior
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time a trustedForwarder is live, including during pause events

operational_questions: Is payGaslessBasefee ever callable independently of a successful meta-tx transfer in the deployed forwarder?
Are paymasters blacklisted ever, and can they still receive basefees?
During incident response, do operators expect pause() to stop *all* token movements?
validation_requirements: Simulate paused state and attempt payGaslessBasefee via trusted forwarder; confirm it succeeds
Check whether the Forwarder always calls payGaslessBasefee only after successful transfer (and cannot be made to call it otherwise)

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Ensure/target environment where a trustedForwarder is set
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Cause forwarder to invoke payGaslessBasefee with payer=victim, paymaster=attacker
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Repeat to drain victim balance in increments of _gaslessBasefee (if forwarder allows arbitrary calls)

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Partial (fee siphoning) if forwarder is misbehaving
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Pause/blacklist controls ineffective for basefee transfers; compliance breach possible
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium: rotate trusted forwarder; potentially reimburse victims
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Gasless transaction accounting and incident response

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: High leverage during pause events and blacklist enforcement periods

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: GaslessBasefeePaid events during paused() == true; basefee paid from blacklisted payer
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Basefee payments without corresponding Forwarder.execute transfer events
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Repeated basefee charges from same payer to same paymaster

validation_result.impact_summary: payGaslessBasefee bypasses all adminSanity controls (pause, blacklist), allowing token movements from/to blacklisted addresses and during paused state when called by the trusted forwarder.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The payGaslessBasefee function in Token.sol performs a token transfer via _update(payer, paymaster, _gaslessBasefee) without calling adminSanity(). Every other value-moving path in the token (transfer, transferFrom, transferWithAuthorization, forceTransfer) routes through adminSanity which enforces pause and blacklist checks. This function only checks isTrustedForwarder(msg.sender) and balance sufficiency. While in the normal Forwarder.execute() flow, a preceding transfer() call would fail if paused (causing the entire execute to revert before reaching payGaslessBasefee), a compromised or alternative trusted forwarder contract could call payGaslessBasefee directly, bypassing all safety controls. This is a code defect: the function is missing mandatory safety checks that all other transfer paths enforce.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Token.sol payGaslessBasefee clearly calls _update() without any adminSanity check. Compare with transfer() at Token.sol which calls transferSanity() -> adminSanity() before any _update. The forceTransfer() in ERC20AdminUpgradeable.sol also calls adminSanity() before _update(). payGaslessBasefee is the only value-moving function that skips these checks entirely.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Calls _update(payer, paymaster, _gaslessBasefee) without calling adminSanity(payer, paymaster). This means: (1) paused() is not checked - tokens move during pause, (2) isBlacklisted(payer) is not checked - blacklisted payers can have tokens taken, (3) isBlacklisted(paymaster) is not checked - blacklisted addresses can receive tokens, (4) to == address(this) is not checked.

This is the check that payGaslessBasefee should call but doesn't. All other transfer paths (transfer, transferFrom, transferWithAuthorization, forceTransfer) call this function before executing _update().
Shows the pattern all other transfer functions follow: calling adminSanity before proceeding. payGaslessBasefee breaks this pattern.

calling_sequence: 1. Attacker deploys malicious contract or compromises existing trusted forwarder
3. isTrustedForwarder(msg.sender) passes because msg.sender is the set trusted forwarder
4. Balance check passes if victim has >= _gaslessBasefee tokens
6. No pause check, no blacklist check on either payer or paymaster
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The entry is payGaslessBasefee in Token.sol. The only guard is isTrustedForwarder(msg.sender) which checks msg.sender == _trustedForwarder (ERC20MetaTxUpgradeable.sol isTrustedForwarder). If msg.sender matches, the function proceeds directly to _update() (inherited from ERC20Upgradeable) which is the low-level balance manipulation function. No adminSanity() call is made anywhere in this path. In the normal Forwarder.execute() flow (Forwarder.sol execute function), a transfer() is called first which would revert if paused, causing execute to revert before reaching payGaslessBasefee. But a compromised or alternative forwarder can call payGaslessBasefee independently.

deployment_assumptions_checked: A trusted forwarder is set (non-zero) and can call payGaslessBasefee
The forwarder can be induced to call payGaslessBasefee in contexts that are not paired with a successful user-intended transfer
config_validation.deployment_evidence: deployToken.js deploys the proxy and sets the owner but does NOT set a trusted forwarder or deploy a Forwarder contract. No script calls setTrustedForwarder(). The ADMIN can set this post-deployment via setTrustedForwarder() which requires onlyRole(ADMIN). The Forwarder.sol exists in the codebase, indicating gasless transactions are an intended feature.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack requires a trusted forwarder to be set. Once set, there are two scenarios: (1) If the legitimate Forwarder is compromised, it can call payGaslessBasefee with arbitrary payer/paymaster during pause or with blacklisted addresses. (2) If ADMIN is compromised, they can set a malicious contract as trusted forwarder which calls payGaslessBasefee in a loop to drain victim balances at _gaslessBasefee per call, bypassing all pause and blacklist controls. Even without compromise, this creates a compliance gap: the contract's pause mechanism does not halt ALL token movements as operators would expect.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity code defect. The missing adminSanity check in payGaslessBasefee means: (1) During incident response pauses, basefee token movements can still occur if a compromised forwarder calls this function directly. (2) Blacklisted/sanctioned addresses can both send (as payer) and receive (as paymaster) tokens through this path. (3) For a regulated EUR stablecoin, this is a compliance violation - the blacklist and pause controls exist for regulatory reasons and are ineffective for this code path. The defect exists in the code regardless of current deployment configuration.
recommended_mitigations: Add adminSanity(payer, paymaster) call at the beginning of payGaslessBasefee, before the _update call
Alternatively, add explicit checks: require(!paused(), 'PausedError'); require(!isBlacklisted(payer), 'SenderBlacklisted'); require(!isBlacklisted(paymaster), 'RecipientBlacklisted');
Consider adding a whenNotPaused modifier to payGaslessBasefee for defense in depth
Add integration tests verifying payGaslessBasefee reverts when contract is paused or when payer/paymaster are blacklisted
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy Token and Forwarder via proxy. 2. Set ADMIN, set trusted forwarder to Forwarder address, set _gaslessBasefee > 0. 3. Fund a user account. 4. Pause the contract. 5. Deploy a malicious contract that calls Token.payGaslessBasefee(user, attacker). 6. Set the malicious contract as trusted forwarder (requires ADMIN). 7. Call the malicious contract to invoke payGaslessBasefee. 8. Verify tokens transferred despite pause. 9. Repeat with blacklisted payer and blacklisted paymaster to confirm bypass.
uncertainties: Whether the deployed system will have a trusted forwarder set (scripts are silent on this)
Whether the legitimate Forwarder contract is the only intended forwarder or if alternative implementations are planned
Whether operators treat pause() as a complete halt of all token movements (likely yes for a regulated stablecoin)
additional_testing_needed: Fuzz test: call payGaslessBasefee with paused=true and verify it succeeds (confirming the bug)
Fuzz test: call payGaslessBasefee with blacklisted payer and verify it succeeds
Fuzz test: call payGaslessBasefee with blacklisted paymaster and verify it succeeds
Integration test: deploy malicious forwarder that calls payGaslessBasefee independently of transfer execution
Invariant test: assert that no _update() call bypasses adminSanity checks across all entry points
